You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/forms/resource-delete-modal.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 6 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 49, 58
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: link

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 49):
   48              key="cancel"
   49              variant="link"
   50              onClick={onCancel}

  (line 58):
   57              key="confirm"
   58              variant="danger"
   59              onClick={onDelete}

```

### Fix 2
Line: 66
Rule [family:Modal]:
## Modal Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Modal appendTo={...} backdropClassName={...} disableFocusTrap={...} elementToFocus={...} id={...} isOpen={...} maxWidth={...} onClose={...}>
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

Props that move to child components:
  description -> <ModalHeader />
  help -> <ModalHeader />
  title -> <ModalHeader />
  titleIconVariant -> <ModalHeader />

Removed props (move to child component as children or remove):
  actions -> map to appropriate child component (any)
  bodyAriaLabel -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  bodyAriaRole -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  footer -> ModalFooter (as children, ReactNode)
  hasNoBodyWrapper -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  header -> ModalHeader (as children, ReactNode)
  showClose -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  titleLabel -> map to appropriate child component (string)

Removed children (no longer valid JSX): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Add imports: ModalBody, ModalHeader, ModalFooter from '@patternfly/react-core'

Remove imports (if no longer used): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Deprecated -> v6 migration:
  Old import: @patternfly/react-core
  New import: @patternfly/react-core
Matching props:
New props on replacement:
  backdropClassName: string
Removed props (no replacement equivalent): actions, bodyAriaLabel, bodyAriaRole, description, footer, hasNoBodyWrapper, header, help, showClose, title, titleIconVariant, titleLabel

### Fix 3
Lines: 45, 54
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: div

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 45):
   44          <div className="footer">
   45            <Button
   46              id="cancel-delete-popup-button"

  (line 54):
   53            </Button>
   54            <Button
   55              id="confirm-delete-popup-button"

```

  Matching props (use on `ContentVariants` instead): a, blockquote, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, p, pre, small

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/forms/resource-delete-modal.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

**Fix 1** (Lines 49, 58): `Button.variant` type changed — but the values used are `"link"` (line 49) and `"danger"` (line 58). Both values exist in the new type union. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Line 66): Modal family migration. The Modal has `title`, `footer`, and children (`<TextContent>...</TextContent>`). In v6:
- `title` stays on `<Modal>` → actually per the fix, `title` moves to `<ModalHeader />`. Let me re-read... Yes, "Props that move to child components: title -> <ModalHeader />"
- `footer` is a removed prop → content moves to `<ModalFooter>` as children
- The children (`<TextContent>...</TextContent>`) should be wrapped in `<ModalBody>`
- Need to add `<ModalHeader>` with the title
- Need to import `ModalBody`, `ModalHeader`, `ModalFooter`

**Fix 3** (Lines 45, 54): Button icon-to-prop migration. Looking at the buttons — they contain text children (`{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Delete')}`), NOT icons. There are no icon elements inside these buttons. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 4** (Line 2): `TextVariants` removed → use `ContentVariants`. Replace import and usage.

**Fix 5** (Line 2): `Text` → `Content`. Replace import and JSX usage.

**Fix 6** (Line 2): `TextContent` → `Content`. Replace import and JSX usage.

For Fixes 4, 5, 6 combined: Both `Text` and `TextContent` map to `Content`. So `<TextContent><Text component={TextVariants.p}>...</Text></TextContent>` becomes `<Content component={ContentVariants.p}>...</Content>` (the wrapper `TextContent` is replaced by `Content`, the inner `Text` with `component` prop also maps to `Content` with `component` prop — but they collapse since `Content` replaces both). Actually, looking more carefully: `TextContent` was a wrapper div, and `Text` was the actual text element. In v6, `Content` replaces both. Since `<TextContent>` was just a wrapper, the `<Text component={TextVariants.p}>` becomes `<Content component={ContentVariants.p}>`.

Let me also check the Modal component being imported from `../modals/modal` — this is a custom wrapper, not directly from PatternFly. But Fix 2 still asks us to restructure based on v6 Modal API. The `footer` prop content should move to `<ModalFooter>`, title to `<ModalHeader>`, and body content wrapped in `<ModalBody>`.

This is critical context. The `Modal` import on line 5 of the target file (`import Modal from '../modals/modal'`) is a **custom Modal wrapper** built on top of `react-modal`, NOT the PatternFly Modal. It accepts `title`, `footer`, `children` as its own custom props.

This means **Fix 2** (Modal family migration to add ModalBody/ModalHeader/ModalFooter) does **not apply** — the component being used is not `@patternfly/react-core`'s Modal. The custom modal has its own `title`, `footer`, and `children` handling. Restructuring it with PF v6 `ModalBody`/`ModalHeader`/`ModalFooter` would break the component.

Now let me check if there are companion test files:

No test files. Now let me apply all the changes.

Let me summarize the changes needed:

1. **Fix 1**: `variant="link"` and `variant="danger"` are both still valid in v6. **No change needed.**

2. **Fix 2**: The `Modal` here is a custom wrapper (`../modals/modal`), NOT PatternFly's Modal. The rule matched because the scanner saw `TextContent` inside something called `Modal`, but it's not the PF Modal. **Cannot apply — wrong component.**

3. **Fix 3**: The Buttons at lines 45 and 54 contain only text children (`{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Delete')}`), no icons. **No change needed.**

4. **Fix 4**: Replace `TextVariants` → `ContentVariants` in import and usage (line 67: `TextVariants.p` → `ContentVariants.p`).

5. **Fix 5**: Replace `Text` → `Content` in import and JSX usage (line 67: `<Text component=...>` and line 71: `</Text>`).

6. **Fix 6**: Replace `TextContent` → `Content` in import and JSX usage (line 66: `<TextContent>` and line 72: `</TextContent>`).

For fixes 4+5+6 combined: `TextContent` was a wrapper div, `Text` was the text element with `component` prop. In v6, `Content` replaces both. Since `<TextContent>` just wraps `<Text>`, the result should collapse to a single `<Content component={ContentVariants.p}>`.

The import changes: Remove `Text`, `TextContent`, `TextVariants`; add `Content`, `ContentVariants`.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The values used (`"link"` on line 49, `"danger"` on line 58) both exist in the new type union. No code modification required. |
| **Fix 2** | Modal family migration (`sd-cf-modal-modal-req-wrap`) | **Could not apply** | The `Modal` used in this file is a **custom wrapper** imported from `../modals/modal` (built on `react-modal`), NOT PatternFly's `@patternfly/react-core` Modal. Adding `ModalBody`/`ModalHeader`/`ModalFooter` children would break the custom modal's API. |
| **Fix 3** | Button icon-children-to-prop | **No change needed** | Both `<Button>` instances (lines 45 and 54) contain only text children (`{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Delete')}`), not icon elements. No icon-to-prop migration is applicable. |
| **Fix 4** | `TextVariants` removed → `ContentVariants` | **Applied** | Replaced `TextVariants` with `ContentVariants` in the import (line 2) and in JSX usage (`TextVariants.p` → `ContentVariants.p`). |
| **Fix 5** | `Text` → `Content` | **Applied** | Removed `Text` from imports. Replaced `<Text component={ContentVariants.p}>...</Text>` with `<Content component={ContentVariants.p}>...</Content>`. |
| **Fix 6** | `TextContent` → `Content` | **Applied** | Removed `TextContent` from imports and removed the `<TextContent>` wrapper element. Since both `TextContent` (wrapper) and `Text` (text element) map to `Content`, the two-level `<TextContent><Text component=...>` collapsed into a single `<Content component={ContentVariants.p}>`. Added `Content` to imports (already added for Fix 5). |

| **Fix 4** | `TextVariants` removed → `ContentVariants` | **Applied** | Replaced `TextVariants` with `ContentVariants` in the import (line 2) and in JSX usage (`TextVariants.p` → `ContentVariants.p`). |
| **Fix 5** | `Text` → `Content` | **Applied** | Removed `Text` from imports. Replaced `<Text component={ContentVariants.p}>...</Text>` with `<Content component={ContentVariants.p}>...</Content>`. |
| **Fix 6** | `TextContent` → `Content` | **Applied** | Removed `TextContent` from imports and removed the `<TextContent>` wrapper element. Since both `TextContent` (wrapper) and `Text` (text element) map to `Content`, the two-level `<TextContent><Text component=...>` collapsed into a single `<Content component={ContentVariants.p}>`. Added `Content` to imports (already added for Fix 5). |
